flop: To fall heavily due to lack of energy.
flow: Movement in people or things characterized with a continuous motion, involving either a non solid mass or a multitude.
flue: A pipe or duct that carries gaseous combustion products away from the point of combustion (such as a furnace).
flux: The act of flowing; a continuous moving on or passing by, as of a flowing stream.
foal: A young horse or other equine, especially just after birth or less than a year old.
foam: A substance composed of a large collection of bubbles or their solidified remains, especially:
foil: A very thin sheet of metal.
fold: To bend (any thin material, such as paper) over so that it comes in contact with itself.
folk: A people; a tribe or nation; the inhabitants of a region, especially the native inhabitants.
fond: Having a liking or affection (for). [ with of]
font: A receptacle in a church for holy water, especially one used in baptism.
food: Any solid substance that can be consumed by living organisms, especially by eating, in order to sustain life.
fool: A person with poor judgment or little intelligence.
foot: A biological structure found in many animals that is used for locomotion and that is frequently a separate organ at the terminal part of the leg.
fora: plural of forum; alternative form of forums
gage: To bind (someone) by pledge or security; to engage.
gait: A manner of walking or stepping; a bearing or carriage while moving on legs.
game: A playful or competitive activity. A playful activity that may be unstructured; an amusement or pastime.
gape: To open the mouth wide, especially involuntarily, as in a yawn, anger, or surprise.
garb: Fashion, style of dressing oneself up.
gasp: To draw in the breath suddenly, as if from a shock.
gate: A doorlike structure outside a house.
gaur: An Asian species of wild bovine (Bos gaurus), of large size and an untamable disposition.
gear: Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.
geek: A carnival performer specializing in bizarre and unappetizing behavior.
gene: A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms which may take several values and (in principle) predetermines a precise trait of an organism's form (phenotype), such as hair color.
germ: The small mass of cells from which a new organism develops; a seed, bud, spore, or zygote. The embryo of a seed, especially of a seed used as a cereal or grain. See Wikipedia article on cereal germ. The small mass of cells from which a part of an organism develops, or a macroscopic but immature form of that part; a bud.
ghat: A descending path or stairway to a river; a ford or landing-place.
ghee: A type of clarified butter used in South Asian cooking.
gift: Something given to another voluntarily, without charge.
gill: A breathing organ of fish and other aquatic animals.
gilt: Gold or other metal in a thin layer; gilding. Gold-colored paint or other coating.
giro: A transfer of funds between different account holders, carried out by the bank according to payer's written instructions.
gist: The main idea or substance, or the most essential part, of a longer or more complicated matter; the crux, the heart, the pith.
give: To move, shift, provide something abstract or concrete to someone or something or somewhere. To transfer one's possession or holding of (something) to (someone).
glee: Joy; happiness; great delight, especially from one's own good fortune or from another's misfortune.
glen: A secluded and narrow valley, especially one with a river running through it; a depression between hills; a dale.
glow: To emit heat and light without a flame.
glue: A hard gelatin made by boiling bones and hides, used in solution as an adhesive; or any sticky adhesive substance.
glued: simple past and past participle of glue
gnat: Any small insect of the order Diptera, specifically within the suborder Nematocera.
goad: A long, pointed stick used to prod animals.
gorge: The front aspect of the neck; the outside of the throat.
grama: Various species of grass in the genus Bouteloua, including Bouteloua gracilis (blue grama)
grit: A collection of hard small materials, such as dirt, ground stone, debris from sandblasting or other such grinding, or swarf from metalworking.
grow: To become larger, to increase in magnitude.
grub: An insect, especially a beetle, at an immature stage of its life cycle.
guan: Any (member) of several species of birds in the genera Aburria, Chamaepetes, Oreophasis, Penelope, Penelopina, and Pipile, of the family Cracidae, limited to the Americas.
gulf: A hollow place in the earth; an abyss; a deep chasm or basin.
gull: A seabird of the genus Larus or of the subfamily Larinae.
guru: In Indian traditions: a spiritual teacher who transmits knowledge to a shishya.
gust: A strong, abrupt rush of wind.
haar: Thick, cold, wet fog along the northeastern coast of Northern England and Scotland.
hack: To chop or cut down in a rough manner.
haft: The handle of a tool or weapon.
hail: Balls or pieces of ice falling as precipitation, often in connection with a thunderstorm.
hair: A pigmented filament of keratin which grows from a follicle on the skin of humans and other mammals.
hajj: The pilgrimage to Mecca made by pious Muslims; the fifth of the five pillars of Islam.
hale: Sound, entire, healthy; robust, not impaired.
half: One of two usually roughly equal parts into which anything may be divided, or considered as divided.
halo: A circular band of coloured light, visible around the sun or moon etc., caused by reflection and refraction of light by ice crystals in the atmosphere.
halt: To limp; move with a limping gait.
hand: The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.
hank: A coil or loop of something, especially twine, yarn, or rope.
hard: Solid and firm. Resistant to pressure; difficult to break, cut, or penetrate.
hare: Any of several plant-eating mammals of the genus Lepus, similar to a rabbit, but larger and with longer ears.
harp: A musical instrument consisting of a body and a curved neck, strung with strings of varying length that are stroked or plucked with the fingers and are vertical to the soundboard when viewed from the end of the body Any instrument of the same musicological type.
hart: A male deer, especially the male of the red deer after his fifth year.
hash: Food, especially meat and potatoes, chopped and mixed together. corn-beef hash
hath: third-person singular simple present indicative of have
haul: To transport by drawing or pulling, as with horses or oxen, or a motor vehicle.
hawk: A diurnal predatory bird of the family Accipitridae, smaller than an eagle.
haze: Very fine solid particles (smoke, dust) or liquid droplets (moisture) suspended in the air, slightly limiting visibility.
head: The part of the body of an animal or human which contains the brain, mouth, and main sense organs.
heal: To make better from a disease, wound, etc.; to revive or cure.
heap: A crowd; a throng; a multitude or great number of people.
hear: To perceive sounds through the ear.
heil: To greet with a Sieg Heil.
hell: A place of torment where some or all sinners are believed to go after death and evil spirits are believed to be.
hijab: A traditional headscarf worn by Muslim women, covering the hair and neck.
hove: To remain suspended in air, water etc.; to float, to hover.
howe: Alternative form of how (a tumulus, a bowl barrow).
howl: The protracted, mournful cry of a dog, wolf or other canid; also of other animals.
hoya: Any plant of the genus Hoya
huff: A heavy breath; a grunt or sigh.
hula: A form of chant and dance, which was developed in the Hawaiian Islands by the Polynesians who originally settled there.
hulk: A large ship used for transportation; a large ship that is difficult to manoeuvre. A non-functional but floating ship, usually stripped of equipment and rigging, and often put to other uses such as accommodation or storage.
hull: The outer covering of a fruit or seed.
hung: simple past and past participle of hang (except when referring to the method of execution; there, hanged is used instead)
hunt: To find or search for an animal in the wild with the intention of killing the animal for its meat or for sport.
hurt: To cause (a person or animal) physical pain and/or injury.
husk: The dry, leafy or stringy exterior of certain vegetables or fruits, which must be removed before eating the meat inside.
hymn: A song of praise or worship, especially a religious one.
hype: Promotion or propaganda, especially exaggerated claims.
ibex: A type of wild mountain goat of the genus Capra, such as the species Capra ibex.
ibis: Any of various long-legged wading birds in the family Threskiornithidae, having long downcurved bills used to probe the mud for prey such as crustaceans.
icon: An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
idea: An abstract archetype of a given thing, compared to which real-life examples are seen as imperfect approximations; pure essence, as opposed to actual examples.
idol: A graven image or representation of anything that is revered, or believed to convey spiritual power.
imam: One who leads the salat prayers in a mosque.
inch: An English unit of length equal to 1/12 of a foot or 2.54 cm, conceived as roughly the width of a thumb.
incur: To bring upon oneself or expose oneself to, especially something inconvenient, harmful, or onerous; to become liable or subject to.
into: To or towards the inside of.
iota: The ninth letter of the Greek alphabet (Ι, ι).
iris: A plant of the genus Iris, common in the northern hemisphere, and generally having attractive blooms (See Iris (plant) on Wikipedia.Wikipedia ).
iron: A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel: a chemical element having atomic number 26 and symbol Fe.
isle: An island, usually very small. Compare with islet.
itch: A sensation felt on an area of the skin that causes a person or animal to want to scratch said area.
jury: A group of individuals chosen from the general population to hear and decide a case in a court of law.
jute: The coarse, strong fibre of the East Indian plants, Corchorus olitorius and Corchorus capsularis, used to make mats, paper, gunny cloth etc.
kain: poultry, etc., required by the lease to be paid in kind by a tenant to the landlord.
kaka: Any of four taxa of birds in the genus Nestor in the parrot family confined to New Zealand and adjacent islands.
kale: An edible plant, similar to cabbage, with curled leaves that do not form a dense head (Brassica oleracea var. acephala)
kami: An animistic god or spirit in the Shinto religion of Japan.
kana: The hiragana and katakana syllabaries. These are made up of characters that represent individual syllables, which are used to write Japanese words and particles. Kana are derived from kanji.
karoo: Any vast prairie bordering a desert.
kata: Any of a sequence of positions and movements used in many martial arts.
kava: A plant from the South Pacific, Piper methysticum.
keck: To heave or retch as if to vomit.
keel: A large beam along the underside of a ship’s hull from bow to stern.
keen: Often with a prepositional phrase, or with to and an infinitive: showing a quick and ardent responsiveness or willingness; eager, enthusiastic, interested.
keep: To continue in (a course or mode of action); to not intermit or fall from; to uphold or maintain.
kelly: A square or hexagonal pipe that is turned in order to rotate the rotary table of a drilling rig.
kelp: Any of several large brown algae seaweeds (especially of order Laminariales).
kemp: A coarse or bristly hair, whisker; knotty hairs that will not felt.
kent: simple past and past participle of ken
kept: simple past and past participle of keep
khan: A ruler over various Turkic and Mongol peoples in the Middle Ages.
kick: To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.
kill: To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
kiln: An oven or furnace or a heated chamber, for the purpose of hardening, burning, calcining or drying anything; for example, firing ceramics, curing or preserving tobacco, or drying grain.
kilo: Alternative letter-case form of Kilo of the ICAO/NATO radiotelephony alphabet.
kilt: To gather up (skirts) around the body.
kind: A type, race or category; a group of entities that have common characteristics such that they may be grouped together.
king: A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy; in an absolute monarchy, the supreme ruler of his nation.
kino: A botanical gum produced by various trees and other plants, particularly bloodwood species of eucalypts (Angophora, Corymbia, Eucalyptus), Butea, and Pterocarpus, used in tanning and dyeing and as an astringent in medicine.
kiss: To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
kite: A bird of prey of the family Accipitridae.
kiva: A ceremonial underground chamber in a Pueblo village.
kiwi: A flightless bird of the order Apterygiformes native to New Zealand.
knee: In humans, the joint or the region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank.
knit: To turn thread or yarn into a piece of fabric by forming loops that are pulled through each other. This can be done by hand with needles or by machine.
knob: A rounded protuberance, especially one arising from a flat surface; a fleshy lump or caruncle.
knot: A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.
know: To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of; to be certain that.
kobo: A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Nigerian naira.
kohl: A dark powder, usually powdered stibnite (an antimony ore), used as eye makeup, especially in Eastern countries.

last: Final, ultimate, coming after all others of its kind.
late: Near the end of a period of time.
laura: A number of hermitages or cells in the same neighborhood occupied by anchorites who were under the same superior
lava: The molten rock ejected by a volcano from a vent such as a crater or fissure; magma that has breached the surface of the earth.
lawn: Ground (generally in front of or around a house) covered with grass kept closely mown.
lazy: Unwilling to do work or make an effort; disinclined to exertion.
lead: A heavy, pliable, inelastic metal element, having a bright, bluish color, but easily tarnished; both malleable and ductile, though with little tenacity. It is easily fusible, forms alloys with other metals, and is an ingredient of solder and type metal. Atomic number 82, symbol Pb (from Latin plumbum).
leaf: The usually green and flat organ that represents the most prominent feature of most vegetative plants.
leak: A crack, crevice, fissure, or hole which admits water or other fluid, or lets it escape.

leer: To look sideways or obliquely; now especially with sexual desire or malicious intent.

lend: To allow to be used by someone temporarily, on condition that it or its equivalent will be returned.
leno: A type of weave used to make light, open cotton fabric used for window curtains.
lest: For fear that; that not; in order to prevent something from happening; in case.

liang: Synonym of tael, a former Chinese unit of weight (about 40 g) and a related unit of silver currency.
liar: Someone who tells a lie; especially, a person who frequently lies.
lido: Part of the sea by a beach sectioned off for swimming and other aquatic activities.

lier: A person or thing that lies, in the sense of being horizontal.

like: To enjoy, be pleased by; favor; be in favor of.
lima: Alternative letter-case form of Lima of the ICAO/NATO radiotelephony alphabet.

lulu: A remarkable person, object, or idea.

luna: A luna moth: a member of species Actias luna.
lune: A fit of lunacy or madness; a period of frenzy; a crazy or unreasonable freak.

lure: Something that tempts or attracts, especially one with a promise of reward or pleasure.

Any instrument of the same musicological classification; any yoke lute.
maar: A broad volcanic crater, usually filled with water to form a lake.

mage: A magician, wizard, sorcerer, witch, warlock or mystic.
maid: A girl or an unmarried young woman; maiden.
main: Of chief or leading importance; prime, principal.
mair: One of various former royal officials in the Kingdom of Scotland.
make: To create. To build, construct, produce, or originate.
male: Belonging to the sex which typically produces sperm, or to the gender which is typically associated with it.
mall: A pedestrianised street, especially a shopping precinct.

many: before a countable noun: A large, indefinite number of.

Precious metal, especially gold or silver. A measure of the inertia of a mass of matter, one of four fundamental properties of matter. SI unit of mass: kilogram. A medicinal substance made into a cohesive, homogeneous lump, of consistency suitable for making pills.
mast: A tall, slim post or tower, usually tapering upward, used to support, for example, sails or observation platforms on a ship, the main rotor of a helicopter, flags, floodlights, meteorological instruments, or communications equipment, such as an aerial, usually supported by guy-wires (except in the case of a helicopter).

mecca: Alternative form of Mecca. ; a place to which a group of people (for example shoppers) are drawn.

migs: ISO 639-3 language code for San Miguel el Grande Mixtec.
mile: The international mile: a unit of length precisely equal to 1.609344 kilometers established by treaty among Anglophone nations in 1959, divided into 5,280 feet or 1,760 yards.

milky: Resembling milk in color, consistency, smell, etc.; consisting of milk.

milt: The spleen, especially of an animal bred for food.

mimic: To imitate, especially in order to ridicule.
mine: That or those belonging to me. Used predicatively.
mined: That has been mined (in various senses).

mips: ISO 639-3 language code for Apasco-Apoala Mixtec.
mirza: An educated man in India or Iran (Persia); an official, a clerk.
miss: To fail to hit, catch, grasp, etc.
missy: A young female, or miss; as a term of mild disparagement, typically used jokingly or rebukingly.
mist: Water or other liquid finely suspended in air.

mixed: simple past and past participle of mix

mock: An imitation, usually of lesser quality.
modal: Of, or relating to a mode or modus.

mogul: A rich or powerful person; a magnate, nabob.
moist: Characterized by the presence of moisture; not dry; slightly wet; damp.
molar: A back tooth having a broad surface used for grinding one's food.

molly: A woman or girl, especially of low status or prostitute.

mondo: A dialogue between master and student designed to obtain an intuitive truth.

mono: A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.

mood: A mental or emotional state, composure.
moody: Given to sudden or frequent changes of mind; temperamental.
moon: Alternative letter-case form of Moon (“the Earth's only permanent natural satellite”).
moor: An extensive waste covered with patches of heath, and having a poor, light (and usually acidic) soil, but sometimes marshy, and abounding in peat; a heath.

moot: Subject to discussion (originally at a moot); arguable, debatable, unsolved or impossible to solve.
mora: A delay in bringing a claim.
moral: Of or relating to principles of right and wrong in behaviour, especially for teaching right behaviour.
moray: Any of the large cosmopolitan carnivorous eels of the family Muraenidae.
more: comparative degree of many: in greater number.
morph: A recurrent distinctive sound or sequence of sounds representing an indivisible morphological form; especially as representing a morpheme.
morse: A clasp or fastening used to fasten a cope in the front, usually decorative.
mort: Death; especially, the death of game in hunting.

motte: A raised earth mound, often topped with a wooden or stone structure and surrounded with a ditch.

mucus: A slippery secretion from the lining of the mucous membranes.
muddy: Covered or splashed with, or full of, mud (“wet soil”).

nancy: An effeminate man, especially a homosexual.
nasal: Of or pertaining to the nose or to the nasion.
naval: Of or relating to a navy.
nawab: A Muslim official in South Asia acting as a provincial deputy ruler under the Mughal empire; a local governor.
neem: Azadirachta indica, a large, mostly evergreen tree from India, whose seeds yield neem oil, the insecticide azadirachtin.
nerve: A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
never: At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.
newer: comparative form of new: more new, more recent.
newly: Very recently/lately; in the immediate past.
nexus: A form or state of connection.
niche: A cavity, hollow, or recess, generally within the thickness of a wall, for a statue, bust, or other erect ornament.
niece: A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother ("fraternal niece"), or of one's sister ("sororal niece").
night: The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.

noble: An aristocrat; one of aristocratic blood.

noir: Of or pertaining to film noir, or the atmosphere associated with that genre

noisy: Making a noise, especially a loud unpleasant sound

nome: A prefecture or unit of regional government in Greece.
nomen: The family name of an Ancient Roman, designating their gens.

tack: A small nail with a flat head.
taco: A Mexican snack food made of a small tortilla (soft- or hard-shelled) filled with ingredients such as meat, rice, beans, cheese, diced vegetables, and salsa.
tact: Sensitive mental touch; special skill or faculty; keen perception or discernment; ready power of appreciating and doing what is required by circumstances; the ability to say the right thing and avoid statements that will give offence or pain even if true.
taiga: A subarctic zone of evergreen coniferous forests situated south of the tundras and north of the steppes in the Northern Hemisphere.
tail: The caudal appendage of an animal that is attached to their posterior and near the anus or cloaca.
taka: The official currency of Bangladesh, equal to 100 paisas. Symbol: ৳
take: To get into one's hands, possession, or control, with or without force.
taken: Infatuated; fond of or attracted to.
tala: The currency of Samoa, divided into 100 sene.
tale: A rehearsal of what has occurred; narrative; discourse; statement; history; story.
talk: To communicate, usually by means of speech.
tall: Having a top that is far up (contrast: deep ('having a bottom that is far down' ).) Having great vertical extent (usually reckoned from the bottom of the feet to the very top of the head) when standing. (For instance, somebody with a height of over 6 feet would generally be deemed tall.)
talon: A sharp, hooked claw of a bird of prey or other predatory animal.
taluk: A hereditary estate in parts of India; subsequently, an administrative subdivision of a district.
tammy: A kind of woolen, or woolen and cotton, cloth, often highly glazed, used for curtains, sieves, strainers, etc.
tang: A refreshingly sharp aroma or flavor.
tango: Alternative letter-case form of Tango of the ICAO/NATO radiotelephony alphabet.
tank: A closed container for liquids or gases.
tarot: A card game played in various different variations.
taste: One of the sensations produced by the tongue in response to certain chemicals; the quality of giving this sensation.
tawny: Of a light brown to brownish orange colour; orangey brown tinged with gold.
taxed: simple past and past participle of tax
taxon: A group of one or more populations of an organism or organisms treated as a unit by taxonomists.
tenor: A musical range or section higher than bass and lower than alto.
tense: The property of indicating the point in time at which an action or state of being occurs or exists.
tenth: The ordinal numeral form of ten; next in order after that which is ninth.
terra: A geologic and geographic region of a celestial body which is a highland analogous to a continent or large landmass.
terry: A type of coarse cotton fabric covered in many small raised loops that is used to make towels, bathrobes and some types of nappy/diaper.
tesla: In the International System of Units, the derived unit of magnetic flux density or magnetic inductivity.
thane: A rank of nobility in pre-Norman England, roughly equivalent to baron.
thank: singular of thanks (“an expression of appreciation or gratitude; grateful feelings or thoughts; favour, goodwill, graciousness”)
their: Belonging to, from, of, or relating to, them (plural).
theme: A subject, now especially of a talk or an artistic piece; a topic.
there: In or at a place or location (stated, implied or otherwise indicated) that is perceived to be away from, or at a relative distance from, the speaker (compare here).
theta: The eighth letter of the Modern Greek alphabet, ninth in Old Greek: Θ, θ.
thick: Relatively great in extent from one surface to the opposite in its smallest solid dimension.
thief: One who carries out a theft.
thigh: The upper leg of a human, between the hip and the knee.
thing: That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.
think: To ponder, to go over in one's mind.
third: The ordinal form of the cardinal number three; Coming after the second.
thong: A narrow strip of material, typically leather, used to fasten, bind, or secure objects.
thorn: A modified branch that is hard and sharp like a spike.
three: NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 3.
throw: To hurl; to release (an object) with some force from one’s hands, an apparatus, etc. so that it moves rapidly through the air.
thumb: The shortest and thickest digit of the hand that for humans has the most mobility and can be made to oppose (moved to touch) all of the other fingers.
tibia: The inner and usually the larger of the two bones of the leg or hind limb below the knee, the shinbone.
tiger: Panthera tigris, a large predatory mammal of the cat family, indigenous to Asia.
tight: Firmly held together; compact; not loose or open.
timer: Agent noun of time: someone or something that times. A device used to measure amounts of time.
tinge: A small added amount of colour; a small added amount of some other thing.
tired: simple past and past participle of tire
titan: Something or someone of very large stature, greatness, or godliness.
title: The name of a film, musical piece, painting, or other work of art.
toast: Bread that has been toasted (cooked lightly by browning).
today: On the current day or date.
token: Something serving as an expression of something else.
tola: A unit of mass used in India, equal to the mass of a silver rupee coin, fixed at 180 troy grains (11.663 8038 grams) in 1833, and of a similar but slightly variable value before that date.
tommy: A British infantryman, especially one from World War I. Ellipsis of Tommy Atkins.
tonal: Of or relating to tones or tonality.
tonga: A light, two-wheeled, horse-drawn carriage used for transportation in India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh.
tonic: Pertaining to tension, especially of the muscles.
tonne: A unit of mass equal to 1000 kilograms.
tooth: A hard, calcareous structure present in the mouth of many vertebrate animals, generally used for biting and chewing food.
torch: A stick of wood or plant fibres twisted together, with one end soaked in a flammable substance such as resin or tallow and set on fire, which is held in the hand, put into a wall bracket, or stuck into the ground, and used chiefly as a light source.
torso: The main part of the (human) body that extends from the neck to the groin, excluding the head and limbs.
tort: A wrongful act, whether intentional or negligent, regarded as non-criminal and unrelated to a contract, which causes an injury and can be remedied in civil court, usually through the awarding of damages.
torus: The standard representation of such a space in 3-dimensional Euclidean space: a surface or solid formed by rotating a closed curve, especially a circle, about a line which lies in the same plane but does not intersect it (e.g. like a ring doughnut).
toss: A throw, a lob, of a ball etc., with an initial upward direction, particularly with a lack of care.
total: An amount obtained by the addition of smaller amounts.
totem: Any natural object or living creature that serves as an emblem of a tribe, clan or family; the representation of such an object or creature.
touch: Primarily physical senses. To make physical contact with; to bring the hand, finger or other part of the body into contact with.
tour: A journey through a particular building, estate, country, etc.
tout: Someone advertising for customers in an aggressive way.
towed: simple past and past participle of tow
towel: A cloth used for wiping, especially one used for drying anything wet, such as a person after a bath.
town: A settlement; an area with residential districts, shops and amenities, and its own local government; especially one larger than a village and smaller than a city, historically enclosed by a fence or walls, with total populations ranging from several hundred to more than a hundred thousand (as of the early 21st century)
tram: A passenger vehicle for public use that runs on tracks in the road (called a streetcar or trolley in North America).
trap: A machine or other device designed to catch (and sometimes kill) animals, either by holding them in a container, or by catching hold of part of the body.
tray: A small, typically rectangular or round, flat, and rigid object upon which things are carried.
tree: A perennial woody plant taller and larger than a shrub with a wooden trunk and, at some distance from the ground, having leaves and branches.
tres: A three-course stringed instrument similar to a guitar; the Cuban variant has six strings, and the Puerto Rican has nine.
trey: A playing card or die with the rank of three.
trim: To reduce slightly; to cut; especially, to remove excess.
trio: A group of three people or things.
trip: A journey; an excursion or jaunt.
trot: A gait of a person or animal faster than a walk but slower than a run.
troy: Of, or relating to, troy weight.
true: Conforming to the actual state of reality or fact; factually correct.
tsar: An emperor of Russia (1547 to 1917) and of some South Slavic states.
tuba: A large brass musical instrument, usually in the bass range, played through a vibration of the lips upon the mouthpiece and fingering of the keys.
tube: Anything that is hollow and cylindrical in shape.
tuck: To pull or gather up (an item of fabric).
tufa: Calcareous lime deposited by precipitation from a body of water, such as a hot spring.
tuff: A light porous rock, now especially a rock composed of compacted volcanic ash varying in size from fine sand to coarse gravel.
tuft: A bunch of feathers, grass or hair, etc., held together at the base.
tule: Any of a number of large freshwater sedges of western North America formerly classified in the genus Scirpus, but now mostly as Schoenoplectus
tuna: Any of several species of fish of the genus Thunnus in the family Scombridae.
turf: A layer of earth covered with grass; sod.
turn: To make a non-linear physical movement. To move about an axis through itself.
tusk: One of a pair of elongated pointed teeth (either cuspids or incisors) that extend outside the mouth of an animal such as walrus, elephant or wild boar, and which continue to grow throughout the animal's life.
tutu: A ballet skirt made of layered stiff but light netting.
twig: A small thin branch of a tree or bush.
twin: Either of two people (or, less commonly, animals) who shared the same uterus at the same time; one who was born at the same birth as a sibling.
type: A grouping based on shared characteristics; a class.
tyre: The ring-shaped protective covering around a wheel which is usually made of rubber or plastic composite and is either pneumatic or solid.
ursa: A bear, especially as used in constellation names such as Ursa Major and Ursa Minor.
vara: A traditional Spanish unit of length, equivalent to about 83.7 cm.
vest: A sleeveless garment that buttons down the front, worn over a shirt, and often as part of a suit; a waistcoat.
veto: A political right to disapprove of (and thereby stop) the process of a decision, a law etc.
vial: A glass or plastic vessel or bottle, especially a small tube-shaped bottle used to store medicine, perfume or other chemicals.
vibe: An atmosphere or aura felt to belong to a person, place or thing.
vice: Bad or immoral behaviour. (Especially often, a habit that harms oneself or others)
vied: simple past and past participle of vie
view: Visual perception. The act of seeing or looking at something.
vine: Any plant whose stem requires support and which climbs by tendrils or twining.
viol: A stringed instrument related to the violin family, but held in the lap between the legs like a cello, usually with C-holes, a flat back, a fretted neck and six strings, played with an underhanded bow hold.
visa: A permit to enter and leave a country, normally issued by the authorities of the country to be visited.
vita: A hagiography; a biography of a saint.
viva: Long live ... ! (used to express acclaim or support).
void: Containing nothing; empty; not occupied or filled.
vole: Any of a large number of species of small rodents of the tribes Arvicolini, Ellobiusini, Clethrionomyini, Pliomyini, Phenacomyini and Prometheomyini.
volt: In the International System of Units, the derived unit of electrical potential and electromotive force (voltage); the potential difference across a conductor when a current of one ampere uses one watt of power. Symbol: V
vote: A formalized choice on legally relevant measures such as employment or appointment to office or a proceeding about a legal dispute.
wade: To walk through water or something that impedes progress.
wadi: A valley, gully, or stream bed in northern Africa and southwest Asia that remains dry except during the rainy season.
wage: An amount of money paid to a worker for a specified quantity of work, usually calculated on an hourly basis and expressed in an amount of money per hour.
wain: A wagon; a four-wheeled cart for hauling loads, usually pulled by horses or oxen.
wait: To delay movement or action until some event or time; to remain neglected or in readiness.
wake: (often followed by up) To stop sleeping.
walk: To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.
wall: A rampart of earth, stones etc. built up for defensive purposes.
wand: A hand-held narrow rod, usually used for pointing or instructing, or as a traditional emblem of authority.
wane: A gradual diminution in power, value, intensity etc.
want: To wish for or desire (something); to feel a need or desire for; to crave, hanker, or demand.
ward: A warden; a guard; a guardian or watchman.
ware: Goods or a type of goods offered for sale or use.
warm: Of a somewhat high temperature, often but not always connoting that the high temperature is pleasant rather than uncomfortable.
warn: To make (someone) aware of (something impending); especially: To make (someone) aware of impending danger, evil, etc.
warp: The state, quality, or condition of being twisted, physically or mentally: The state, quality, or condition of being physically bent or twisted out of shape. The state, quality, or condition of being deviant from what is right or proper morally or mentally.
wary: Cautious of danger; carefully watching and guarding against deception, trickery, and dangers; suspiciously prudent
wife: A married woman, especially in relation to her spouse.
wile: A trick or stratagem practiced for ensnaring or deception; a sly, insidious artifice
will: Used to express the future tense, sometimes with an implication of volition or determination when used in the first person. Compare shall.
wilt: To droop or become limp and flaccid (as a dying leaf or flower).
wind: Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.
wine: An alcoholic beverage made by fermenting grape juice, with an ABV ranging from 5.5–16%.
wing: An appendage of an animal's (bird, bat, insect) body that enables it to fly.
wink: To close one's eyes in sleep.
wipe: To move an object over, maintaining contact, with the intention of removing some substance from the surface.
wire: Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.
wise: Showing good judgement or the benefit of experience.
wish: A desire, hope, or longing for something or for something to happen.
wold: An unforested or deforested plain, a grassland, a moor.
wolf: Canis lupus; the largest wild member of the canine subfamily.
womb: In female mammals, the organ in which the young are conceived and grow until birth; the uterus.
wood: The substance making up the central part of the trunk and branches of a tree. Used as a material for construction, to manufacture various items, etc. or as fuel.
wool: The hair of the sheep, llama and some other ruminants.
word: The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. The smallest discrete unit of spoken language with a particular meaning, composed of one or more phonemes and one or more morphemes The smallest discrete unit of written language with a particular meaning, composed of one or more letters or symbols and one or more morphemes A discrete, meaningful unit of language approved by an authority or native speaker (compare non-word). A sign used in a sign language.
worm: A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.
worn: Damaged and shabby as a result of much use.
wort: Now chiefly as the second element in the names of plants: a plant used for food or medicine.
wrap: To enclose (an object) completely in any flexible, thin material such as fabric or paper.
wren: Troglodytes troglodytes (Eurasian wren, type species and sole Old World species of the family Troglodytidae).
writ: A written order, issued by a court, ordering someone to do (or stop doing) something.
wynn: A letter of the Old English alphabet, ƿ, borrowed from the futhark and used to represent the sound of w; replaced in Middle English times by the digraph uu, which later developed into the letter w.
yang: A principle in Chinese and related East Asian philosophies associated with bright, hot, masculine, etc. elements of the natural world.
yank: A sudden, vigorous pull (sometimes defined as mass times jerk, or rate of change of force).
yard: A small, usually uncultivated area adjoining or (now especially) within the precincts of a house or other building.
yarn: A twisted strand of fibre used for knitting or weaving.
yeah: Used to express acknowledgment of what was previously said.
year: A period of time akin to the time taken for the Earth to undergo a full cycle of seasons.
yell: To shout; holler; make a loud sound with the voice, especially to express intense emotions such as anger or excitement.
yeti: An unidentified humanoid animal said to live in the Himalayas.
your: Belonging to you; of you; related to you (singular; one owner).
zona: A zone or band; a layer.
